title: LinearCombination
summary: Defines finite linear combinations of multilinear ambient operators and their evaluation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Defines finite linear combinations of multilinear ambient operators and their evaluation.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/LinearCombination.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A single scalar multiple of a rank-r operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, c in the real numbers, and p of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is Cal Conf.multi Smul c P.
correspondence.leanType: def singleOperator {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A finite linear combination of rank-r operators. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, ι a type, s a finite set, c of type ι maps to the real numbers, and p of type ι maps to cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is fun F U maps to ∑ i belongs to S, c i times P i F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{operatorSum}(S,c,P)=\operatorname{fun} F U => \sum i \in S, c i \cdot P i F U
correspondence.leanType: def operatorSum {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, c in the real numbers, P of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, Cal Conf.single Operator c P F U equals c times P F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;c\colon \mathbb{R},\;P\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{singleOperator} c P F U = c \cdot P F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem singleOperator_apply {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.singleOperator c P F U = c * P F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, ι a type, S a finite set, c of type ι maps to the real numbers, P of type ι maps to Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, Cal Conf.operator Sum S c P F U equals ∑ i belongs to S, c i times P i F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;ι\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;S\colon \operatorname{Finset} ι,\;c\colon ι \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;P\colon ι \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{operatorSum} S c P F U = \sum i \in S, c i \cdot P i F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_apply {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.operatorSum S c P F U = ∑ i ∈ S, c i * P i F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, ι a type, c of type ι maps to the real numbers, and P of type ι maps to Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, Cal Conf.operator Sum ∅ : Finset ι c P equals Cal Conf.multi Zero r.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;ι\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;c\colon ι \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;P\colon ι \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{operatorSum} (∅ \colon \operatorname{Finset} ι) c P = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiZero} r
correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_empty {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.operatorSum (∅ : Finset ι) c P = CalConf.multiZero r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, ι a type, i of type ι, c of type ι maps to the real numbers, and P of type ι maps to Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, Cal Conf.operator Sum i : Finset ι c P equals Cal Conf.single Operator c i P i.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;ι\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;i\colon ι,\;c\colon ι \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;P\colon ι \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{operatorSum} ({i} \colon \operatorname{Finset} ι) c P = \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{singleOperator} (c i) (P i)
correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum_singleton {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (i : ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.operatorSum ({i} : Finset ι) c P = CalConf.singleOperator (c i) (P i)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Boundary equivalence is stable under finite sums. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,ι\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;S\colon \operatorname{Finset} ι,\;A,B\colon ι \longrightarrow \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;hAB\colon \forall i \in S, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (A i) (B i),\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{SameBoundaryValue} (U \longmapsto \sum i \in S, A i U) (U \longmapsto \sum i \in S, B i U)
correspondence.leanType: theorem SameBoundaryValue_finset_sum {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (A B : ι → Function Conf) (hAB : ∀ i ∈ S, CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (A i) (B i)) : CalConf.SameBoundaryValue (fun U => ∑ i ∈ S, A i U) (fun U => ∑ i ∈ S, B i U)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: A finite linear combination of tangential operators is tangential. In symbols, the proposition is the statement displayed below.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;ι\colon \mathrm{Type} _,\;\operatorname{DecidableEq} ι,\;S\colon \operatorname{Finset} ι,\;c\colon ι \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;P\colon ι \longrightarrow \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;w\colon \operatorname{Fin} r \longrightarrow \mathbb{R},\;hP\colon \forall i \in S, \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (P i) w,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{IsTangentialAtWeights} (\operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{operatorSum} S c P) w
correspondence.leanType: theorem operatorSum {r : ℕ} {ι : Type _} [DecidableEq ι] (S : Finset ι) (c : ι → ℝ) (P : ι → CalConf.MultiOperator r) {w : Fin r → ℝ} (hP : ∀ i ∈ S, CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (P i) w) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeights (CalConf.operatorSum S c P) w

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
